input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition that checks `$(IsTestProject)`, which the MSTest SDK sets automatically when `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or `MSTest` is referenced:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <!-- shared properties for all projects -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** The MSTest/NUnit/xUnit SDKs set `IsTestProject=true` during evaluation for any project that references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. Your library projects never reference it, so the condition is `false` and they stay as libraries.

**If `IsTestProject` isn't set early enough** (rare, depends on import order), a reliable fallback is to match on project name convention:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
```

The `IsTestProject` approach is preferred because it's convention-independent and officially supported by the .NET SDK.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conventions. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the MSTest runner package reference, or more commonly, to use a naming convention or check for `IsTestProject`.

The cleanest approach for MTP migration is to condition on `$(IsTestProject)` which MSTest SDK sets automatically, or to use a naming convention like checking if the project name ends with `.Tests`.


I'll provide a straightforward, practical solution for this.
